objective: Refresh governance policy across STANDARDS.md, hldpro-sim process personas, and the new functional acceptance auditor agent so that model-routing rules are session-agnostic (Claude CLI and Codex CLI both compliant), Tier 1 planning authority is dual-family (both anthropic and openai families authorized for creation AND review), QA cross-family is mandatory, the fallback ladder explicitly fails up (not down), and every slice terminates in a structured PASS/FAIL functional acceptance audit artifact produced by functional-acceptance-auditor. Delivered as three atomic slices: F (#651 STANDARDS.md rewrite), G (#652 hldpro-sim governance process personas + AnthropicApiProvider), H (#653 functional-acceptance-auditor agent + schema + CI integration). Slice H depends on Slice F.
scope_boundary: STANDARDS.md sections SoM (Society of Minds / model routing) and PDCAR (Plan-Do-Check-Act-Review) — Slice F only.
packages/hldpro-sim/process-agents/ subtree (new schema and 5 persona JSON files) and hldprosim/{personas.py,providers.py} — Slice G only.

agents/functional-acceptance-auditor.md, docs/schemas/functional-acceptance-audit.schema.json, raw/acceptance-audits/.gitkeep, AGENT_REGISTRY.md, tests/test_functional_acceptance_auditor.py — Slice H only.
Cross-review artifacts under raw/cross-review/ (one per slice; dual-signed Anthropic + OpenAI).
Functional acceptance audit artifacts under raw/acceptance-audits/ (one per slice).
Model-fallback degradation logs under raw/model-fallbacks/ when primary models are unavailable.
out_of_scope: Workflow file changes (.github/workflows/) — no edits in any slice; cross-review trigger regex from Slice C is already authoritative.
Hook script changes (.claude/hooks/, hooks/) — no edits in any slice.
Downstream consumer repos (ai-integration-services, HealthcarePlatform, knocktracker, local-ai-machine, seek-and-ponder) — no writes; downstream adoption tracked separately.

Retiring or modifying agents/verify-completion.md — the new functional-acceptance-auditor is additive (post-implementation behavioral gate), not a replacement for verify-completion (pre-session structural gate).
Issue #614 implementation work — Slice G must verify non-overlap before branch cut but does not subsume #614.
Refactor of existing process personas in packages/hldpro-sim/personas/ — Slice G adds process-agents/ as a new sibling directory; existing personas untouched.
Cost-threshold or rate-limit fallback policy beyond logging the trigger — fallback executors and budget gates are out of scope.
research_summary: Epic #650 codifies governance rules that must hold across BOTH Claude CLI and Codex CLI sessions (session-agnostic). Three orthogonal concerns require atomic slicing: (1) the policy text itself in STANDARDS.md (Slice F) must drop hardcoded model-family pins from rule text, add an explicit family column to the routing table, document fallup direction, list dual Tier 1 planners, prohibit same-family QA, expand the worker table to include codex-spark and codex-5.4-medium, and reference the new auditor gate in §PDCAR; (2) hldpro-sim must be able to simulate the governance loop end-to-end (Slice G) by introducing a process-persona schema distinct from the existing role-personas, five concrete personas (Tier 1 planner, Tier 1 plan reviewer cross-family, Tier 2 worker, Tier 3 QA cross-family, Tier 4 functional acceptance auditor), and an AnthropicApiProvider that mirrors the existing OpenAiApiProvider for live-API tests gated by ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; (3) the functional acceptance auditor (Slice H) is a Tier 2 quality-gate agent (haiku, fallup to sonnet) that EXECUTES hooks and smoke tests referenced by each slice's AC, asserts intended behavior (not absence of error), and emits a strict-schema PASS/FAIL artifact. Slice H depends on F because STANDARDS.md §PDCAR must reference the auditor by name (AC-H8) and that reference is added in F (AC-F6). F and G are independent and may run in parallel. Cross-review trigger regex from Slice C will fire automatically on F (STANDARDS.md) and H (agents/, schema/).

sprints.name: Slice F - Issue #651 - STANDARDS.md policy rewrite
sprints.goal: Rewrite STANDARDS.md §SoM and §PDCAR so that model routing is session-agnostic, fallback direction is fail-up (not down), Tier 1 planning authority is dual-family, QA cross-family is mandatory, the worker roster includes codex-spark and codex-5.4-medium, and the functional acceptance auditor is documented as the required final AC gate on every slice. No workflow, hook, or script changes.
tasks: Cut branch issue-651-slice-f-standards-rewrite-20260501 from origin/main@9d570b8.
Rewrite STANDARDS.md §SoM rule text to eliminate hardcoded model family names; move all model pins into the routing table with an explicit Family column (anthropic|openai).
Update routing table Tier 1 row to list both claude-opus-4.X (anthropic) and Codex 5.X high (openai) as valid planners for creation AND review.
Update routing table Tier 2 worker row to include codex-spark and codex-5.4-medium alongside claude-sonnet-4.X.

Add explicit fallup rule to the fallback ladder: unavailable primary escalates UP to a more capable model; codex-spark unavailable -> gpt-5.4 medium; claude-opus-4 unavailable -> log degraded independence, require operator acknowledgment before falling to sonnet for Tier 1; all degraded fallbacks logged to raw/model-fallbacks/.
Add explicit rule: same-family QA is prohibited; Tier 3 QA must be cross-family from the Tier 2 worker.
Add explicit text to §PDCAR: every slice final acceptance criterion is spawning functional-acceptance-auditor which must return overall_verdict=PASS before closeout proceeds; reference agent by name.
Confirm SOM-BOOTSTRAP-001 string absent from STANDARDS.md and all .github/workflows/*.yml via grep (read-only; no workflow edits).
Produce dual-signed cross-review artifact at raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-slice-f-standards-rewrite.md.
Spawn functional-acceptance-auditor on the Slice F branch; require PASS at raw/acceptance-audits/2026-05-01-651-functional-audit.json before closeout.
Open PR for Slice F; Stage 6 closeout.
acceptance_criteria: AC-F1: STANDARDS.md §SoM rule text contains no hardcoded model family names outside the routing table; verified by grep for claude|opus|sonnet|haiku|gpt|codex in §SoM narrative paragraphs returning only routing-table or family-column references.
AC-F2: STANDARDS.md §SoM fallback ladder explicitly contains fallup direction: unavailable primary escalates to more capable, log degraded independence.

AC-F3: STANDARDS.md routing table Tier 1 row lists both anthropic-family and openai-family Tier 1 planners with explicit create AND review authority; verified by: grep -E 'Tier 1|planner' STANDARDS.md | grep -i 'anthropic\|openai' | grep -c 'review' returning >= 1.
AC-F4: STANDARDS.md contains an explicit sentence prohibiting same-family QA.

AC-F5: STANDARDS.md routing table Tier 2 worker row contains at least three worker entries spanning both anthropic and openai model families; verified by: grep -A5 'Tier 2\|worker' STANDARDS.md | grep -cE 'spark|sonnet|medium' returning >= 2.
AC-F6: STANDARDS.md §PDCAR documents the functional acceptance auditor as required final step on all slices, referencing agent by name functional-acceptance-auditor.
AC-F7: grep SOM-BOOTSTRAP-001 across STANDARDS.md and .github/workflows/ returns zero matches.

AC-F8: raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-slice-f-standards-rewrite.md exists and is dual-signed; verified by: grep -c 'Signature:' raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-slice-f-standards-rewrite.md returning >= 2, and grep -c 'anthropic' raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-slice-f-standards-rewrite.md returning >= 1, and grep -c 'openai' raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-slice-f-standards-rewrite.md returning >= 1.

AC-F9: raw/acceptance-audits/2026-05-01-651-functional-audit.json exists with overall_verdict=PASS produced by functional-acceptance-auditor per agents/functional-acceptance-auditor.md protocol; verified by: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('raw/acceptance-audits/2026-05-01-651-functional-audit.json')); assert d['overall_verdict']=='PASS' and 'functional-acceptance-auditor' in d.get('auditor_model','') + d.get('auditor_signature','')" exiting 0.

sprints.name: Slice G - Issue #652 - hldpro-sim governance process personas + AnthropicApiProvider
sprints.goal: Introduce a process-persona schema and five concrete process-persona JSON files in packages/hldpro-sim/process-agents/ for the governance loop roles; update PersonaLoader to search process-agents/ first; implement AnthropicApiProvider with extended thinking, strict JSON schema, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY enforcement. Independent of Slice F; runs in parallel.
tasks: Verify non-overlap with open issue #614 via gh issue view 614; cross-comment on #652 confirming scope separation BEFORE cutting the branch.
Cut branch issue-652-slice-g-hldpro-sim-personas-20260501 from origin/main@9d570b8.

Author packages/hldpro-sim/process-agents/governance-process-persona.schema.json (JSON Schema draft 2020-12) with required fields: persona_id, role_name, description, tier (1-4 integer), primary_model, primary_model_family (anthropic|openai), reasoning_effort (low|medium|high), fallback_model, fallback_trigger (api_unavailable|rate_limit|cost_threshold|operator_override|manual_request), input_schema (object: type + schema_ref), output_schema (object: type + schema_ref), constraints (array of string).

Implement AnthropicApiProvider in hldprosim/providers.py: reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, raises ValueError if unset; uses anthropic SDK with extended thinking; enforces strict JSON schema in response.

Author docs/schemas/functional-acceptance-audit.schema.json (JSON Schema draft 2020-12) with required: audit_id (pattern YYYY-MM-DD-<issue>-functional-audit), issue_number (integer), slice_branch (string), audited_at (date-time), acceptance_criteria (array: ac_id, description, verification_command, exit_code, actual_output_excerpt, verdict PASS|FAIL), hook_results (array: hook_path, exit_code, stdout_excerpt, stderr_excerpt), smoke_test_results (array: test_id, command, expected_outcome, actual_outcome, verdict), evidence_artifacts (array of string), overall_verdict (enum PASS|FAIL|BLOCKED), auditor_model (string), auditor_signature (string).

All 8 checklist items pass: slices atomic, H->F dependency enforceable, all ACs machine-verifiable, functional-acceptance-auditor gate explicit on F/G/H, dual-signature pattern correct, no family leakage, fallup documented, same-family QA prohibition documented.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Three atomic slices: F (#651 STANDARDS.md rewrite — independent), G (#652 hldpro-sim process personas + AnthropicApiProvider — independent, gated on #614 non-overlap), H (#653 functional-acceptance-auditor — depends on F). Every slice ends with dual-signed cross-review and PASS-verdict functional acceptance audit.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Cross-family plan review by gpt-5.4 high produces raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-issue-650-plan-review.md. On accepted status, orchestrator creates per-slice execution scopes and dispatches F and G in parallel, holding H until F merges.
blocked_on: gpt-5.4 high cross-family plan review — alternate_model_review status currently not_requested.
Slice G dispatch held until #614 non-overlap is documented on issue #652.
Slice H is BLOCKED on Slice F merge (#651 → origin/main) before branch may be cut.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Plan validates against docs/schemas/structured-agent-cycle-plan.schema.json with zero errors.
Cross-family Tier 1 plan review (gpt-5.4 high) returns accepted or accepted_with_followup at raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-issue-650-plan-review.md.
Per-slice execution scopes created under raw/execution-scopes/2026-05-01-issue-65{1,2,3}-*.json before worker dispatch.
Slice dependency honored at dispatch: F and G fan out in parallel; H held until F merges.
All three slices produce dual-signed cross-review artifacts AND PASS-verdict functional acceptance audits before closeout.
Worker selection at dispatch honors fallup direction; degraded fallbacks logged to raw/model-fallbacks/.
QA reviewer for each slice is cross-family from that slice's worker.

material_deviation_rules: Any change to file_paths beyond scope_boundary requires opening a new governance issue and re-approval; mid-slice scope creep into workflows/, hooks/, or scripts/ is a HARD STOP.
If Slice F lands without the explicit string 'functional-acceptance-auditor' in §PDCAR, Slice H is BLOCKED — AC-H8 cannot pass; remediate before Slice H may proceed.
If Slice G discovers genuine overlap with #614, halt the slice, reconcile via comment chain on both issues, and re-plan; do NOT silently merge overlapping work.
If the functional-acceptance-auditor returns overall_verdict=FAIL or BLOCKED on any slice, do NOT close the slice; remediate the failing AC and re-run the auditor.
Same-family QA is a HARD STOP — if no cross-family QA reviewer is available, log under raw/model-fallbacks/ and HALT until operator acknowledgment.
Fallback direction violations (falling DOWN instead of UP) are material deviations; record under raw/model-fallbacks/ and require operator override before continuing.
Cross-review artifact missing or singly-signed is a HARD STOP at AC-{F8,G8,H9}.

Plan must itself comply with the rules it encodes: rule text in this plan naming a specific model family outside an explicit routing/family-column context is a self-violation and a material deviation.
approved_by: gpt-5.4 high (Tier 1 plan reviewer, openai family; accepted iteration 2 after plan patch)
